title: Tit For Tat
content:

        Have you snared a weeping hare?
    Have you whistled, 'No Nunny,'and gunned a poor bunny,

title: The Mermaids
Keep Innocency
content:
Like an old battle, youth is wild
 With bugle and spear, and counter cry,
 Fanfare and drummery, yet a child
 Dreaming of that sweet chivalry,

title: Time Passes
content:
There was nought in the Valley
     But a Tower of Ivory,
 Its base enwreathed with red
     Flowers that at evening

title: Grim
content:
Beside the blaze of forty fires
        Giant Grim doth sit,
    Roasting a thick-woolled mountain sheep
        Upon an iron spit.
    Above

title: Sadly, O, Sadly
content:
Sadly, O, sadly, the sweet bells of Baddeley
     Played in their steeples when Robin was gone,
                 Killed by an arrow,

title: Nocturne
content:
'Tis not my voice now speaks; but a bird
 In darkling forest hollows a sweet throat - 
 Pleads on till distant echo too hath heard
     And double

title: The Thief At Robin's Castle
content:
There came a Thief one night to Robin's Castle,
    He climbed up into a Tree;
    And sitting with his head among the branches,
    A wondrous

title: The Blind Boy
content:
"I have no master," said the Blind Boy,
    "My mother, 'Dame Venus' they do call;
 Cowled in this hood she sent me begging
    For whate'er in pi

title: The Fly
content:
How large unto the tiny fly
        Must little things appear! - 
     A rosebud like a feather bed,
        Its prickle like a spear;

title: Thule
content:
If thou art sweet as they are sad
    Who on the shores of Time's salt sea
 Watch on the dim horizon fade
    Ships bearing love to night and thee

title: Be Angry Now No More
content:
Be angry now no more!
 If I have grieved thee - if
 Thy kindness, mine before,
 No hope may now restore:
 Only forgive, forgive!

title: April Moon
content:
Roses are sweet to smell and see,
    And lilies on the stem;
 But rarer, stranger buds there be,
    And she was like to them.

 The little moon

title: Dust To Dust
content:
Heavenly Archer, bend thy bow;
 Now the flame of life burns low,
 Youth is gone; I, too, would go.

 Even Fortune leads to this:
 Harsh or kind,

title: To Margot
content:
When I go free,
 I think 'twill be
 A night of stars and snow,
 And the wild fires of frost shall light
 My footsteps as I go;
 Nobody - nobody

title: Age
content:
This ugly old crone - 
 Every beauty she had
 When a maid, when a maid.
 Her beautiful eyes,
 Too youthful, too wise,
 Seemed ever to come
 To

title: For All The Grief
content:

    May now a few clear flowers blow,
 In the dust, and the heat, and the silence of birds,

title: Estranged
content:

 Happy I was - alone;
 Yet from the sunshine suddenly
    A joy was gone.

 A bird in an empty house

title: The Buckle

     I sewed it on my shoe,
     And 'neath a sprig of mistletoe
     I danced the evening through!

title: Falstaff

 And leaned rheumatic rafters o'er his head - 
 A blowzed, prodigious man, which talked, and stared,

content:

content:

        As odd as can be - -
    That whatever Miss T. eats

title: Silver

    This way, and that, she peers and sees

    All beautiful to see,

    A hope - half memory:

Captain Lean
content:

        Swept down on Captain Lean - 
     That mariner and gentleman
        Will ne'er again be seen.

     He

title: Clear Eyes
content:

    And cheeks outlive their rose.
 Time, heedless of the past,
    No loving-kindness knows;
 Chill unto mortal lip

title: Myself
content:

    With mists of autumntide;
 Under the giant boughs,
    Stretched green on every side,

 Along the lonely paths,

title: The Mocking Fairy

Wanderers
content:

    Tossing their lovely dews,
    Lustrous and fair;

title: The Mountains
content:

 The icy hills far off from me
 With frosty ulys overgrown
 Stand in their sculptured secrecy.

 No path

    The fire burns faint and low,

    Into grey ashes go;
 I strive to read,

content:

title: Home
content:

 Until the quiet grave

title: Hamlet

 He sat, his eyes companioned with dream -

title: Unstooping

title: An Epitaph
content:
Here lies a most beautiful lady,
 Light of step and heart was she;
 I think she was the most beautiful lady
 That ever was in the West Country.

title: Imogen
content:

 All mute humanity's last simpleness, - 
 And yet the roses in her cheeks unfallen!

    In her snowy white apron,

content:

     Gropes for worms

 In the starry darkness of night

 Or, haply, is it fading dreams
     Are in my eyes?

 Not even an echo in my

    Seemingly lost in cloudless Paradise,

    All the hours of life I see,
    Since my foolish nurse did once
    Bed me on her leggen bones;

title: The Exile

        And all across the green
    Came scampering in, on wing and claw,
        Chicken fat and lean:

content:
I saw sweet Poetry turn troubled eyes
    On shaggy Science nosing in the grass,
    For by that way poor Poetry must pass
 On her long pilgrimage

content:

     Mouse in wainscot, Saint in niche -

title: Mrs. Macqueen (Or The Lollie-Shop)
content:

    Down on the cobbles
    Lives Mrs. MacQueen,

title: The Vacant Day
content:
As I did walk in meadows green
    I heard the summer noon resound
 With call of myriad things unseen
    That leapt and crept upon the ground.

title: To My Mother
content:
Thine is my all, how little when 'tis told
         Beside thy gold!
 Thine the first peace, and mine the livelong strife;
 Thine the clear dawn,

title: Where Is Thy Victory?

 When the unclean earth covers me;
 Only in surety if thou cry

    And ever so many people go,
    Past the small lodge, forlorn and still,
    Under the heavy branches, till

title: The Dunce
content:
Why does he still keep ticking?
        Why does his round white face
    Stare at me over the books and ink,
        And mock at my disgrace?

title: Nobody Knows
content:

        By the ivied orchard wall,
    Over the leaves in the dark night,

 To some sweet riddle in his mind that seemed
     A-tiptoe into song.

 In hi

content:
Where the bluebells and the wind are,
        Fairies in a ring I spied,
     And I heard a little linnet
        Singing near beside.

content:

 Who, with bright Fear's lean taper, crossed a hand

     On the roofs, walls, belfries

    Now his day is done,
 And all his children
    Far away are gone.

 He sits beneath his jasmined porch,

content:

        O for a lanthorn green!
     For those sweet stars the Pleiades,
     That glitter in the twilight tree

content:

    But Mrs. Earth can not stain gold,
        Nor ruby red.

 Actionless, full of visions, soon
     Vanishing, soon sped.

content:

    The ugliest shop of all is his,

title: Fear
content:

 The eyes of Fear;
 I, I alone,
 Where shadowy-clear,
 Watching for me,
 Lurks Fear.

 'Tis ever still

content:
Child, do you love the flower
    Ashine with colour and dew
 Lighting its transient hour?
     So I love you.

title: The Window

    The people passing - passing by;
    And not a single one can see
        My tiny watching eye.

title: There Blooms No Bud In May
content:
There blooms no bud in May
 Can for its white compare
 With snow at break of day,
 On fields forlorn and bare.

 For shadow it hath rose,
 Azure

title: The Moth
content:

    Musked with the dark's faint bloom,
    Out into glooming and secret haunts
        The flame cries, 'Come!'

 Puffed to the huge circumference of a sigh,
 But past all tinge of apples long ago.

    My tired thoughts to rest:
 No peace of lovelier valleys made
    Like peace within my breast.

 Thine

content:

 Memory may still dwell on
 Her beauty shadowed,
 And the sweet smell gone.

 That vanishing loveliness,
 That burdening

title: Down-Adown-Derry
content:

        In the meadows of Doone,
     Hears a shrill piping,

title: The Disguise
content:

 Dead is my well-content,
 And buried deep my pride.
 Cold are their stones, beloved,
 To h

title: The Massacre
content:

    Lay in that lake of sun,
 As I with my little sword went in - 
    Against a thousand, one.

 Haughty and infinit

title: The Supper
content:
A wolf he pricks with eyes of fire
     Across the night's o'ercrusted snows,
         Seeking his prey,
         He pads his way
     Where

title: To E.T.: 1917

title: The Riddlers

 Linnet and Blackcap, Woodlark, Thrush,

title: The Flight
content:
How do the days press on, and lay
    Their fallen locks at evening down,
 Whileas the stars in darkness play
     And moonbeams weave a crown -

title: Vain Questioning
content:

title: Humanity
content:

    To flaunt the purple of the Universe,
    To strut and strut, and thy great part rehearse;
 Ever the slave

title: Sam
content:
When Sam goes back in memory,
        It is to where the sea
     Breaks on the shingle, emerald-green,
        In white foam, endlessly;

title: The Fool Rings His Bells
The Silver Penny
content:

        My bright silver penny,
     If out to sea you'll sail me
        And my dear sister Jenny.'

title: Winter
content:
Clouded with snow
    The cold winds blow,
 And shrill on leafless bough
 The robin with its burning breast
    Alone sings now.

    The ray

title: The Market-Place
content:
My mind is like a clamorous market-place.
    All day in wind, rain, sun, its babel wells;
    Voice answering to voice in tumult swells.
 Chaffer

title: Old Susan
content:
When Susan's work was done she'd sit,
 With one fat guttering candle lit,
 And window opened wide to win
 The sweet night air to enter in;
 There

title: Alas, Alack!
content:

    There's a fish that talks
        In the frying-pan.
    Out of the fat,

content:

    Capped with a golden helm,
 Sate in the ages, in his floating ship,
     In a dead calm.

title: Alulvan

content:

    And a cup of sack
        His thirst to slake;
    Bird in arras

title: The Double
content:
I curtseyed to the dovecote.
        I curtseyed to the well.
     I twirled me round and round about,
        The morning sweets to smell.

title: Sleepyhead
content:
As I lay awake in the white moonlight
     I heard a faint singing in the wood,
                 "Out of bed,
                 Sleepyhead,

title: Arabia
content:
Far are the shades of Arabia,
 Where the Princes ride at noon,
 'Mid the verdurous vales and thickets,
 Under the ghost of the moon;

title: Spring
content:

 I, too, with Spring's bright face
 By mine, walked softly along,
    Pace to his pace.

 Then burned his crimson ma

title: The Englishman
content:
I met a sailor in the woods,
        A silver ring wore he,
     His hair hung black, his eyes shone blue,
        And thus he said to me: -

title: Haunted
content:

Will Ever?
content:

content:

        In coats of red,
    Rode their horses
        Up to bed.

    Three jolly gentlemen

content:

        For to bring my Mammie to,
    In a hat with a long feather,
        And a trailing gown of blue;

title: All That's Past
content:
Very old are the woods;
    And the buds that break
 Out of the briar's boughs,
    When March winds wake,
 So old with their beauty are -

title: Silence
content:

    Yet striving for release
    The most delighting string's
     Sweet jargonings,
     The happi

title: The Rainbow
content:

    Of Rainbow span the sky,
 The gold sun burning
    As the rain swept by.

 In bright-ringed solitude
    The showery f

content:

content:

 Beneath its vast-boughed trees!
 Not one trembling leaflet cries
 To that Watcher in the skies -

 Wrinkled with age, and drenched with dew

title: Hide And Seek
Alone
content:

 The squeak of the cricket,
 The stir of the mouse,
 Are all she knows
 Of the earth and us.

 Once she

     Along the hedges
        From Weep to Wicking.
     Half a pottle -

 We, who in our strange kiss
 Have proved a dream the world's realities,

    In its emerald shade,

     My bed should be of ivory,

title: John Mouldy
content:
I spied John Mouldy in his cellar,
     Deep down twenty steps of stone;
     In the dusk he sat a-smiling,
        Smiling there alone.

title: The Enchanted Hill
content:
From height of noon, remote and still,

     No mist, no wind, above, below;
     No living thing strays

Men all, and birds, and creeping beasts,
 When the dark of night is deep,
 From the moving wonder of their lives
 Commit themselves to sleep.

     From its weary body close-enwrapt in slumber

        A hundred years ago,

content:

 Her pale face bent
    In the candle-light,

title: Trees
content:
Of all the trees in England,
        Her sweet three corners in,
    Only the Ash, the bonnie Ash
        Burns fierce while it is green.

content:

    She felt that she must creep,
 So silent was the clear cool house,
    It seemed a house of sleep.
 And sure

The Quarry
content:

 By no wild hope of force or greed

 Toward the dark of this old window

content:

    With a teeny tiny key,
    And there's a jar of Lollypops
         For me, me, me.

 The dusk was still with cold and gloom,

 B

 That in its darkness rocked Orion's stars;

     Did all their hues of summer don,
     But came a wind and troubled them,
        And all were gone.

title: Dream-Song
content:

        As she did dance -

Longlegs

        And all across the combe
     Shrill and shrill it rang - rang through
        The clear green gloom.

title: Full Moon
content:
One night as Dick lay half asleep,
        Into his drowsy eyes
    A great still light begins to creep
        From out the silent skies.

title: The Hawthorn Hath A Deathly Smell

content:
A twangling harp for Mary,
        A silvery flute for John,
     And now we'll play the livelong day,
        'The Miller and his Son.'

content:

            'Out of bed,
            Sleepyhead,

title: Mistress Fell
content:
"Whom seek you here, sweet Mistress Fell?"
 "One who loved me passing well.
 Dark his eye, wild his face - 
 Stranger, if in this lonely place

title: Bunches Of Grapes
Even In The Grave
content:
I laid my inventory at the hand
    Of Death, who in his gloomy arbour sate;
    And while he conned it, sweet and desolate
 I heard Love singing

title: The Listeners
content:
'Is there anybody there?' said the Traveller,
    Knocking on the moonlit door;
 And his horse in the silence champed the grasses
    Of the fores

title: April
content:
Come, then, with showers; I love thy cloudy face
    Gilded with splendour of the sunbeam thro'
    The heedless glory of thy locks. I know
 The a

title: The Witch
content:
Weary went the old Witch,
        Weary of her pack,
     She sat her down by the churchyard wall,
        And jerked it off her back.
